positioning: Obesity Killer Kit is a doctor-formulated Ayurvedic obesity and weight-loss system for overweight and obese adults who want fast visible weight-loss support in a natural, practical, structured way.
It is a structured, guided system with routine, food guidance, tracker, booklet, and WhatsApp support; it is not positioned as a loose supplement or a standalone fat-burner claim product.
Mechanism boundary: protocol-based appetite regulation + eating-behavior correction + digestion support.
Core flow: lower unnecessary intake -> improved caloric-deficit adherence -> body uses stored fat over time.
Public-facing mechanism positioning must stay tied to weight loss or obesity reduction, not abstract routine language alone.
promise_claims: Core promise: 3 to 5 kg weight loss in 15 days when the protocol is followed properly.
Supports fast visible weight-loss progress through a natural, easy-to-follow process that does not leave the user feeling hungry, weak, or overwhelmed.
Supports weight-loss efforts by reducing hunger and cravings, improving satiety, supporting digestive comfort, and making caloric-deficit adherence easier in real life.
Expected outcome language should stay conditional on adherence and eligibility.
eligibility: Primary fit: overweight or obese adults wanting natural, guided, easy-to-follow weight-loss support with fast visible results.
Common fit profiles include users struggling with stubborn fat, belly fat, cravings, emotional eating, low energy, slow metabolism, and repeated diet failure.
Relevant obesity-linked context may include PCOD, PCOS, thyroid imbalance, high blood pressure, fatty liver, diabetes, and IBS, but this must not become a cure claim.
exclusions: Do not use for pregnant women.
Do not use for women within 6 months postpartum.
Do not use for people actively taking insulin.
Do not position for users below 18 years of age.
caution_conditions: Doctor review required before use for people on multiple psychiatric medications.
Doctor review required before use for people with multiple surgery history.
Internal operational handling from FAQ should not override the above eligibility boundaries.
protocol_morning: Take OK Liquid first thing on waking on an empty stomach.
Morning step is the main appetite-support component.
Dried amla is included for post-liquid taste balance and digestive support.
protocol_night: Take OK Tablet plus 1/2 tsp OK Powder around 15 minutes before sleep.
Take the night components with warm water.
Night components are the digestion-support lane, not the main appetite lane.
fasting_rules: After OK Liquid, maintain a 4-hour no-solid-food window.
Zero-calorie drinks are allowed in the fasting window.
Eat only on true hunger; there is no fixed meal timing.
Many users naturally move to 1 to 1.5 meals per day.
allowed_items: If still hungry, up to 30g complex carbs such as millets or oats.
Around 4 liters of water per day.
5000 to 7000 steps per day or pranayam.
Homemade-food compatibility can be used as a benefit lane, but it must not become the whole brand center.
restricted_items: Dairy except milk in tea or coffee.
mechanism_allowed_claims: OK Liquid supports fullness and reduces cravings.
OK Liquid helps shift users from clock-based eating to true-hunger eating.
OK Liquid supports lower unnecessary intake without hunger-led weakness.
OK Tablet and OK Powder support digestive comfort, including bloating, acidity, gas, heaviness, digestion, absorption, and elimination comfort.
System effect: reduced meal frequency, reduced portion size, easier routine consistency.
Supports practical obesity and weight-loss efforts.

Approved mechanism framing must explicitly tie to goal language such as appetite control for weight loss, digestion support for easier weight-loss adherence, or obesity-support routine for fast visible weight-loss progress.
mechanism_disallowed_claims: Do not position the product as a standalone fat-burner.
Do not claim it burns fat rapidly by itself.
Do not claim it melts fat without effort.
Do not claim it works with unlimited junk food.
Do not claim it overrides all discipline.
Do not use abstract-only mechanism lines such as control your routine, reset your day, or transform consistency unless the same unit clearly states weight loss or obesity context.
safety_public_line: Public trust line: no side effects.
safety_internal_handling: Internal handling: first 3 days can be an adjustment phase.
Use public-facing safety language carefully without inventing additional medical safety claims.
Safety reassurance is a protected lane when harsh-method fear is relevant, but it must not replace the weight-loss outcome or mechanism.
guarantee: If weight-loss start is not observed within the first 7 days, full refund can be requested when the protocol was followed as instructed and the daily tracker was shared.
Daily tracker sharing is part of refund eligibility.
Guarantee language should stay tied to conditions and not become an unconditional promise.
continuation: Continue in cycles until target weight or BMI 25.
After every 30 days, take a 7 to 10 day break.
The kit can be repeated, but repeat logic must preserve the 30-day break rule.
maintenance: Maintenance is not lifelong dependency on the kit.
Stopping the kit does not itself cause automatic regain.
Core maintenance logic: maintain caloric balance, follow a balanced diet, stay active, and use optional maintenance support products only if needed.
proof_points: Doctor authority: Dr. Arun Tyagi with 34 years of experience.
31 unique ingredients and 44 total ingredient entries across the kit.
No synthetic ingredients and no chemical preservatives.
messaging_guardrails: Do not use old ingredient counts such as 39 herbs.
Do not make abstract ad copy that hides the obesity or weight-loss objective.
Do not force headline style into old extracted-xlsx patterns; the lane must come from product features first.
Headlines and subheadlines must stay feature-based and outcome-linked, not generic transformation slogans.
Secondary benefits must not overshadow the main promise of fast visible weight loss with a safer, easier, more guided experience.
on_image_copy_requirements: Every headline and subheadline must explicitly mention weight loss, obesity reduction, excess-weight reduction, or clear weight outcome language.
Approved explicit phrases include weight loss support, obesity management support, lose weight, reduce excess weight, 3 to 5 kg in 15 days, and appetite control for weight loss.
Mechanism messaging on-image must explicitly connect to obesity or weight-loss goal, not abstract behavior language alone.
Do not use vague transformational lines without naming weight loss or obesity context.
Every mechanism-led headline/subheadline unit must keep the goal visible in the same unit.
priority_block: Priority 1: lead with the main promise of fast visible weight loss and 3 to 5 kg in 15 days when context allows it.
Priority 2: cravings down, hunger reduction, and easier caloric-deficit adherence are the main physical support benefits and should appear before routine mechanics.
Priority 3: natural safe-feeling, no-side-effects positioning, and all-natural Ayurvedic trust should be used when safety or harsh-method fear is relevant.
Priority 4: homemade-food compatibility and no-separate-meal-prep benefits should be used for time burden and family-food friction, but should not become the whole brand center.
Priority 5: structured system, low guesswork, and clear routine should be used when the pain is confusion, overwhelm, or repeated inconsistency.
Priority 6: guided support means tracker-led guidance, WhatsApp follow-up, and coach support, not vague emotional support copy.
Priority 7: experiential or digestive benefits can support the message, but should not overshadow the main promise.
Priority 8: AM routine and PM routine are mechanism lanes that should be used when the chosen angle genuinely needs morning or night protocol detail, not as the default lead every time.
Priority 9: headlines and subheadlines must stay feature-based and outcome-linked; do not convert them into generic transformation slogans.
Priority 10: if the detailed product doc defines a stronger feature hierarchy or interpretation rule, preserve that detail instead of compressing it.
Priority 11: headline style must not be forced into old extracted-xlsx patterns; the lane must come from product features first.
headline_strategy: Protected priority order for feature-led copy: fast visible results first; cravings down and appetite control for weight loss second; natural safe-feeling and no-side-effects positioning third; homemade-food compatibility and no separate meal prep fourth; structured system and low guesswork fifth; guided support sixth; emotional-control and confidence-restoring support only as support; digestive comfort and secondary internal benefits only as support; AM routine and PM routine only as mechanism lanes when genuinely needed; proof/guarantee only as support.
Headlines must be oriented around concrete product features or product-mechanic outcomes.
Headlines must explicitly name weight loss, obesity reduction, excess-weight reduction, appetite control for weight loss, or a 15-day weight outcome.
Subheadlines must complement the headline with a second feature lane and must not merely restate the headline.
Do not let all ads under the same format repeat the same headline skeleton or the same subheadline skeleton.
Rotate feature lanes across ads in the same format so the set does not collapse into one repetitive routine theme.

Format-level execution: HERO uses one primary feature in the headline and one secondary feature in the support line; UGC allows creator-style practicality but still needs a concrete feature lane; FEAT bullets must each represent different product features; BA shows struggle on left and feature-led shift or progress state on right; TEST review line should reference a specific feature, routine benefit, or outcome driver instead of vague praise.
protected_themes: Fast visible results first.
Cravings down and appetite control for weight loss second.
Natural safe-feeling and no-side-effects positioning third.
Homemade-food compatibility and no separate meal prep fourth.
Structured system and low guesswork fifth.
Guided support sixth.
Emotional-control and confidence-restoring benefits only as support.
Digestive comfort and secondary internal benefits only as support.
AM routine and PM routine only as mechanism lanes when genuinely needed.
Proof and guarantee framing only as support.
trigger_rules: Trigger fast visible results and 3 to 5 kg in 15 days when context allows a performance-led claim.
Trigger cravings down and appetite control for weight loss before routine mechanics when the pain is hunger, snacking, emotional eating, or adherence drop-off.
Trigger natural safe-feeling and no-side-effects positioning when safety concerns, fear of harsh methods, or dependency fear is relevant.
Trigger homemade-food compatibility and no separate meal prep when the pain is time burden, family-food friction, or daily disruption, but do not let it become the brand center.
Trigger structured system and low guesswork when the pain is confusion, inconsistency, or repeated failure from high-friction plans.
Trigger guided support only as tracker-led guidance, WhatsApp follow-up, and coach support; do not turn support into vague emotional reassurance copy.
Trigger AM routine and PM routine only when the chosen angle genuinely needs protocol detail; do not make routine the default lead.
Use proof and guarantee as reinforcement, not as a substitute for stronger feature lanes above.
Use one primary feature lane and one secondary feature lane; do not flatten everything into a single routine theme.

Do not use what-not-to-say violations: body-shaming, cure language, anti-gym framing, competitor insults, abstract transformation lines without explicit weight-loss context, old ingredient counts, or zero-effort/fat-melting claims.

claim_a.text: It is not positioned as a standalone supplement. It is a structured, guided system built for real-life adherence.

claim_b.text: The Obesity Killer Kit is a herbs based food supplement that naturally reduces hunger, creates a calorie deficit, and provides essential nutrients without adding excess calories to your diet.

chosen_claim.text: It is a structured, guided system with routine, food guidance, tracker, booklet, and support; do not position it as a loose supplement or standalone fat-burner claim product.

conflicts.resolution_reason: Single Source of Truth positioning overrides lower-priority FAQ product-classification wording. The FAQ line can be treated as a loose category description, not the approved positioning.

claim_a.text: Adults with BMI above 25 who want natural, guided, easy-to-follow weight-loss support.

claim_b.text: If BMI is below 25, ask them why do they need this course and make them aware that they can lose 2-3kg with the course in the 15 days pack.

chosen_claim.text: Adults 18+ with BMI above 25 are the canonical eligible audience.

conflicts.resolution_reason: The higher-priority product source defines the primary user and eligibility boundary. The FAQ guidance is an operational exception-handling fragment and must not override canonical targeting.

claim_b.text: Yes, our customers on anxiety medications still achieve good results, typically losing 4-5 kg in 30 days.

chosen_claim.text: Doctor review is required before use for people on multiple psychiatric medications.

conflicts.resolution_reason: Higher-priority product caution rules override lower-priority FAQ reassurance. FAQ medication-response language may be used only after the doctor-review boundary is respected.

dropped_claims.text: Do not use old ingredient counts (for example 39 herbs) in internal truth outputs.
Ayurvedic framing: supports pachakagni and dhatvagni, reduces aam accumulation, and supports detoxification.
The course helps you do that in a way that you neither feel hungry nor weak.
We calculate success rate of our customers monthly and last month's (Aug 2025) success rate was 98.3%.
Split 2 pouches of OK Liquid across 3 days. Keep leftover liquid in a glass jar at room temperature.

Do not use what-not-to-say violations: body-shaming, cure language, anti-gym framing, competitor insults, abstract transformation lines without explicit weight-loss context, old ingredient counts, or zero-effort/fat-melting claims.
